methodology: Selection drawn from established histories of medicine, science, rights, and environmental policy — Our World in Data, WHO official records, IUCN, UNESCO archives, Nobel records, and peer-reviewed history. The 'magnitude' field is a coarse estimate of population-scale impact, used only to size the dots on the chart. Where scholarship disagrees on attribution or scale, we publish the range and tag the entry as disputed.

events.name: First powered flight
events.notes: Begins the century of aviation; cumulative reach hard to overstate.

events.name: Yellow fever and malaria control on the Canal
events.notes: First large-scale vector control proving disease could be engineered out of a region.

events.name: Women's suffrage wave (NZ 1893 → US 1920)
events.notes: First national franchise for women; pattern then spread.

events.name: Haber-Bosch process scales
events.notes: Synthetic nitrogen fertilizer; cited as feeding roughly half of humanity by mid-20th century. Counted with environmental tradeoffs.

events.name: Insulin isolated and used in humans
events.notes: Turns a near-universally fatal disease into a managed one.

events.name: Penicillin discovery and mass production
events.notes: Mass-produced from WWII forward; first true antibiotic.

events.name: Universal Declaration of Human Rights
events.notes: Foundational text. Not binding by itself; foundation for treaties that followed.

events.name: Polio vaccines (Salk, Sabin)
events.notes: Cases reduced 99.9% from pre-vaccine era. Eradication of wild types still in progress.

events.name: Decolonization wave
events.notes: Independence of dozens of states from European colonial rule; uneven outcomes but a major rights shift.

events.name: Civil Rights Act + Voting Rights Act (US)
Green Revolution agricultural yields
events.notes: Famine reduction in South Asia widely credited; counted alongside ecological costs.

events.name: Smallpox eradication
events.notes: Only human disease eradicated. ~5M deaths/year averted continuously since.

events.name: ICCPR + ICESCR enter into force
events.notes: Binding human-rights treaty regime.

events.name: Oral rehydration therapy mass adoption
events.notes: Cited as one of the most important medical advances of the 20th century by The Lancet.

events.name: CEDAW adopted and enters into force
Montreal Protocol on ozone-depleting substances
events.notes: Ozone layer projected to recover by 2066; one of the most successful environmental treaties ever signed.

events.name: World Wide Web invented and released royalty-free
events.notes: CERN released W3 patent-free in 1993.

events.name: Ozone layer recovery confirmed on track
events.notes: 2022 assessment confirmed Antarctic ozone hole on track to close by 2066.

events.name: Convention on the Rights of the Child
events.notes: Most widely ratified human-rights treaty in history (196 parties).

events.name: End of apartheid; first multiracial elections (South Africa)
actors: ANC; de Klerk government; SA people

events.name: Global under-5 mortality more than halved
events.notes: From 93 per 1,000 in 1990 to 37 per 1,000 in 2022; roughly 6M fewer child deaths per year.

events.name: Human Genome Project completed (open)
events.notes: HGP kept data open; Celera kept theirs proprietary. The open release set a precedent.

events.name: Global Polio Eradication Initiative
actors: Rotary; WHO; CDC; UNICEF; national governments
events.notes: Cases reduced from ~350,000 in 1988 to a handful per year; 20M people walking who would have been paralyzed.

events.name: Extreme poverty rate falls from ~36% to under 10%
actors: multiple states; especially China, Vietnam, Bangladesh
events.notes: Methodology contested (PPP threshold) but direction agreed; >1B people lifted by World Bank's $2.15 PPP line.

events.name: Global adult literacy reaches ~86%
events.notes: From ~36% in 1950.

events.name: HAART/ART makes HIV a chronic condition
events.notes: AIDS deaths peaked ~2004 and have fallen sharply since.

events.name: Rinderpest declared eradicated
events.notes: Second disease eradicated by humanity; livestock impact lifted millions from food insecurity.

events.name: COVID-19 vaccines developed and distributed
actors: mRNA developers; GAVI; COVAX; national programs
events.notes: Lancet 2022 estimate: 14.4M deaths averted in first year; total likely higher.

events.name: Guinea worm disease near eradicated
events.notes: From ~3.5M cases/year in 1986 to fewer than 15 in 2024.

events.name: Leaded gasoline fully phased out worldwide
actors: UNEP Partnership for Clean Fuels; national bans
events.notes: UNEP estimates ~1.2M premature deaths prevented per year; Algeria last user, 2021.

events.name: Renewables become the cheapest new power source
events.notes: Solar costs fell ~90% in the decade; wind ~70%; coverage by share of new generation.

events.name: Child marriage rates falling
events.notes: Roughly 25M child marriages averted in last decade per UNICEF.

events.name: Threatened species recoveries
events.notes: Humpback whale, bald eagle, California condor, giant panda, Iberian lynx, Mauritius kestrel.

events.name: First gene therapies approved (Luxturna, Zolgensma, Casgevy, Lyfgenia)
events.notes: Casgevy/Lyfgenia (2023) first CRISPR-based therapies for sickle cell.

events.name: GLP-1 agonists scale (semaglutide, tirzepatide)
actors: Novo Nordisk; Eli Lilly; trial programs
events.notes: Cardiovascular and renal endpoints reduced; weight loss outcomes durable.

events.name: Kunming-Montreal 30x30 biodiversity framework
events.notes: Goal: 30% of land and ocean protected by 2030.

events.name: Malaria vaccines (RTS,S then R21) recommended at scale
events.notes: R21 manufacturing scaled to 100M doses/year.
